sentences.text: When a plugin beats asking from scratch
You don't need a plugin for a one-off.
If you're only ever going to do something once, just describe it to Claude — that's faster than hunting for an add-on.

A plugin earns its place when a task is repeated and consistent: the weekly check on your tracker, the same pre-publish review on every client site, the same way of connecting to a tool you use daily.
